chapter_summary: Jesus defends His disciples for picking grain on the Sabbath and heals a man's withered hand on the Sabbath, showing that the Sabbath was made for people. He spends the night in prayer and then names His 12 apostles. He comes down and heals the crowds. He teaches the Beatitudes — blessings for the poor, hungry, and weeping — and calls His followers to love enemies, give generously, forgive, and judge others gently. He ends with the Parable of the Wise and Foolish Builders.
main_lesson: Jesus calls us to a love that is bigger than loving only friends — He wants us to love our enemies, be generous to everyone, and build our lives on His words. A life built on Jesus is a life that stands through every storm.

memory_verse.kjv: And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise.
memory_verse.little_bible: Treat other people exactly the way you want them to treat you. This is the golden rule Jesus taught.

parent_guide: Luke 6 contains Luke's version of the Sermon on the Mount (the 'Sermon on the Plain'). Key teaching sections: (1) Sabbath controversies (v1-11) — Jesus is Lord of the Sabbath; the Sabbath is a gift, not a trap. (2) The Twelve named (v12-16) — note Jesus prays all night before naming them; major decisions follow major prayer. (3) Beatitudes (v20-26) — Luke's version is more stark than Matthew's: actual poor, actual hungry, actually weeping. (4) Love of enemies (v27-36) — the most demanding ethical teaching in Luke; children can be taught: 'Jesus doesn't just want us to love friends — He wants us to love people who are mean to us.' (5) Judge not (v37-42) — the beam and the speck; handle with gentleness not moralism. (6) Tree and fruit (v43-45) — good character produces good actions. (7) Two builders (v46-49) — obedience to Jesus' words is the only secure foundation.
application_for_children: Jesus says to love people even when they are unkind to you — and to treat everyone the way you want to be treated. And if you build your life by listening to Jesus and doing what He says, you will be like a house built on a rock — strong and safe no matter what!

verses.kjv: And it came to pass on the second sabbath after the first, that he went through the corn fields; and his disciples plucked the ears of corn, and did eat, rubbing them in their hands.
verses.little_bible: One Sabbath day, Jesus and His disciples were walking through a grain field. The disciples were hungry, so they picked some grain, rubbed it in their hands, and ate it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus and His friends walked through a field. They picked some grain and ate it.
verses.meaning: God's law allowed people to pick grain from a neighbour's field when hungry. The disciples were doing nothing wrong — but the Pharisees saw a chance to accuse Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus walks with His friends.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You for walking with me through every day.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been really hungry? What did you do?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think the Pharisees were watching Jesus and His disciples so closely?
verses.do_it_today: Go for a walk with someone you love today and thank God for the time together.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and His disciples walking through tall golden grain fields on a bright Sabbath morning, some disciples rubbing grain in their hands and eating, sunlight streaming through the stalks. Peaceful storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And certain of the Pharisees said unto them, Why do ye that which is not lawful to do on the sabbath days?
verses.little_bible: Some Pharisees spotted them and demanded: 'Why are you doing something that isn't allowed on the Sabbath?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Pharisees said: 'You can't do that on the Sabbath!'
verses.meaning: The Pharisees had added hundreds of rules to God's law. Picking grain was fine by God's law, but the Pharisees classified it as harvesting — forbidden work.
verses.memory_phrase: Rules can become traps.
verses.prayer: God, help me love Your real rules and not add burdens for others.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a rule that was meant to help but ended up feeling unfair?
verses.family_discussion: How do we know the difference between God's actual rules and rules people have added on top?
verses.do_it_today: Ask a parent or teacher to explain the reason behind one rule you don't understand.
verses.illustration_prompt: Stern Pharisees in long robes pointing accusingly at the disciples in the grain field, frowning disapprovingly, while the disciples look up in surprise. Warm morning light behind them.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering them said, Have ye not read so much as this, what David did, when himself was an hungred, and they which were with him;
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered them calmly: 'Haven't you even read what King David did when he and his men were hungry?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Have you read what King David did when he was hungry?'
verses.meaning: Jesus answered their challenge with Scripture. He knew the Word of God and used it wisely — not as a weapon but as truth.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knows God's Word.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me learn Your Word so I can use it wisely and well.
verses.discussion_question: Do you have a favourite Bible story? What did you learn from it?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think Jesus pointed to David's example? What was He trying to show the Pharisees?
verses.do_it_today: Read one Bible story today you haven't read before.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing calmly in the grain field, speaking to the Pharisees with one hand raised gently, His face kind but confident. Disciples watching quietly behind Him.

verses.kjv: How he went into the house of God, and did take and eat the shewbread, and gave also to them that were with him; which it is not lawful to eat but for the priests alone?
verses.little_bible: Jesus reminded them: 'David went into God's house and ate the special holy bread that was only for priests — and shared it with his men!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: David ate the special temple bread for priests and shared it with his friends.
verses.meaning: God honoured David even when David broke a technical rule because the need was real. God cares about people, not just ceremonies.
verses.memory_phrase: God cares about people first.
verses.prayer: God, give me a heart that cares about people, not just keeping rules perfectly.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever bent a rule to help someone? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: Does God care more about rules or about people? How do we hold both in balance?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind for someone even when it is inconvenient for you.
verses.illustration_prompt: King David in ancient armour receiving sacred bread in a lamplit tabernacle, handing pieces to exhausted soldiers. Warm golden lamplight, reverent atmosphere. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, That the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said with full authority: 'The Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath itself!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I am Lord over the Sabbath day!'
verses.meaning: Jesus claimed authority over the Sabbath — meaning He is greater than the day, greater than the law, and is in fact its Author. This is a bold claim to be God.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is Lord of everything.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are Lord over every day, every hour, every moment of my life.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be 'Lord' of something? Who is Lord over your life?
verses.family_discussion: If Jesus is Lord of the Sabbath, what does that mean for how we should spend our rest day with Him?
verses.do_it_today: Set aside some special time on your next rest day just to be with Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing tall in the sunlit grain field, arms slightly open, light radiating around Him as He speaks with calm authority. The Pharisees step back, awed and unsettled. Epic storybook style.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass also on another sabbath, that he entered into the synagogue and taught: and there was a man whose right hand was withered.
verses.little_bible: On another Sabbath day, Jesus went to the synagogue to teach. There was a man there whose right hand was shrivelled and couldn't move.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to teach in church. A man there had a hand that didn't work.
verses.meaning: Jesus kept teaching on the Sabbath — and He kept noticing people others ignored. A man's withered hand catches Jesus' attention immediately.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sees people others miss.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me notice people who are hurting that others walk past.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever noticed someone who felt left out? What did you do?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think Luke mentions that it was the man's right hand specifically? What would that mean for his daily life and work?
verses.do_it_today: Today, look for one person who seems to be struggling and say something kind to them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Inside a simple stone synagogue with wooden benches, Jesus teaching, and a man sitting quietly with his right hand held close to his chest. Soft, humble scene. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And the scribes and Pharisees watched him, whether he would heal on the sabbath day; that they might find an accusation against him.
verses.little_bible: The scribes and Pharisees were watching Jesus closely — hoping to catch Him healing on the Sabbath so they could accuse Him of breaking the law.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Pharisees were watching Jesus, hoping to get Him in trouble.
verses.meaning: The religious leaders' hearts were so hardened that a man's suffering was just a trap they wanted to use. They cared more about catching Jesus than helping a hurting man.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't look for reasons to accuse others.
verses.prayer: God, keep my heart soft. Don't let me look for reasons to blame others.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever watched to see if someone would do something wrong? How did it make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: What does it reveal about the Pharisees' hearts that they wanted to use a sick man's suffering as a trap?
verses.do_it_today: Instead of watching for someone to do something wrong today, watch for something good they do and tell them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Scribes and Pharisees sitting stiffly in the synagogue, arms folded, eyes narrowed at Jesus — suspicious, cold. The sick man sits quietly nearby, unnoticed by them. Contrasting warmth and coldness.

verses.kjv: But he knew their thoughts, and said to the man which had the withered hand, Rise up, and stand forth in the midst. And he arose and stood forth.
verses.little_bible: Jesus knew exactly what they were thinking. He looked at the man with the withered hand and said: 'Stand up and come to the middle.' The man stood up and stepped forward.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus knew their plan. He said to the man: 'Come stand in the middle.' The man did.
verses.meaning: Jesus was not intimidated by plots against Him. He saw the man, called him forward, and acted with complete courage — knowing full well what it would cost Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus acts with courage and love.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me be brave and do what is right, even when others might criticise me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever done the right thing even when you knew others might not like it?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knew their thoughts yet still acted. What does that tell us about who He is and what He cares about?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind or right today even if someone might make fun of you for it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking directly and warmly at a man who stands up tentatively in the middle of the synagogue floor, surrounded by watching faces — some curious, some hostile. The man's face shows a flicker of hope.

verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them, I will ask you one thing; Is it lawful on the sabbath days to do good, or to do evil? to save life, or to destroy it?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked everyone: 'Let me ask you one question — is it right to do good on the Sabbath, or to do evil? To save a life, or to destroy one?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'Is it right to do good on the Sabbath or to do harm?'
verses.meaning: Jesus cut through all the religious arguments with one simple question. Doing good is always right. The Sabbath was never meant to prevent love and healing.
verses.memory_phrase: Doing good is always right.
verses.prayer: God, give me courage to do good even when the moment feels complicated.
verses.discussion_question: Is there ever a wrong time to do something kind? Why or why not?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family make sure 'following the rules' never stops you from helping someone who needs it?
verses.do_it_today: Do one good thing for someone today without waiting for a perfect moment.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing with both hands open toward the crowd in the synagogue, a direct, kind, and challenging look on His face. The crowd is silent, caught by the question.

verses.kjv: And looking round about upon them all, he said unto the man, Stretch forth thy hand. And he did so: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
verses.little_bible: Jesus looked around at everyone. Then He said to the man: 'Stretch out your hand.' The man stretched it out — and it was completely healed, just like his other hand!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Stretch out your hand.' The man did, and his hand was healed!
verses.meaning: With just a word, Jesus healed a hand that had never worked. No touch, no medicine — only His command. His word alone has the power to make broken things whole.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' word heals broken things.
verses.prayer: Jesus, speak Your word into the broken places in my life. I trust You to heal.
verses.discussion_question: What is something broken that you wish Jesus would fix?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus only spoke — and healing happened. What does that teach us about the power of Jesus' words?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone who is sick or hurting today and ask Jesus to speak healing into their life.
verses.illustration_prompt: The man stretching out a once-withered hand that is now strong and whole, his face flooding with amazement and joy. Warm golden light in the synagogue. Onlookers gasping. Vivid storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And they were filled with madness; and communed one with another what they might do to Jesus.
verses.little_bible: But the Pharisees and scribes were furious! Instead of celebrating, they huddled together and began plotting what they could do to get rid of Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Pharisees were angry. They made plans against Jesus.
verses.meaning: A miracle of love made the Pharisees' hearts harder, not softer. When people decide not to receive Jesus, no amount of evidence will convince them.
verses.memory_phrase: Keep your heart open to Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, please never let my heart grow hard. Keep me always open and soft toward You.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think seeing a miracle made the Pharisees angrier instead of happier?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean when we see good things happening but instead of being glad, we become upset? What does that show about the heart?
verses.do_it_today: When you see something good happen to someone today, stop and celebrate it with them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Pharisees and scribes huddled outside the synagogue in whispered, angry conversation, backs turned to the light spilling from the doorway. Dark, shadowy tones contrast with the warm light inside.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass in those days, that he went out into a mountain to pray, and continued all night in prayer to God.
verses.little_bible: Around that time, Jesus went up a mountain alone to pray. He prayed all through the night — right until morning.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went up a mountain and prayed to God all night long.
verses.meaning: Before the most important decision of His ministry — choosing His twelve apostles — Jesus spent the entire night in prayer. This is the model for every major decision: pray first, and pray hard.
verses.memory_phrase: Pray before every big decision.
verses.prayer: Jesus, teach me to pray like You did — all night if needed. Big decisions need big prayer.
verses.discussion_question: When you have a big decision to make, what do you usually do? Do you pray first?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus prayed all night before choosing His team. What big decisions in your family life need that kind of prayer?
verses.do_it_today: Before you make one decision today — even a small one — stop and pray about it first.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling alone on a dark mountainside under a sky filled with stars, head bowed, hands folded, the whole world quiet around Him. Moonlight softly illuminating His face. Deeply peaceful storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And when it was day, he called unto him his disciples: and of them he chose twelve, whom also he named apostles;
verses.little_bible: When morning came, Jesus called all His followers together. From all of them, He chose twelve to be His special team — He named them apostles.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: In the morning, Jesus picked twelve special helpers called apostles.
verses.meaning: After a night of prayer, Jesus chose twelve. 'Apostle' means 'one sent out' — these twelve would carry His message to the whole world.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus chose His team after prayer.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to be part of Your team. Use me to share Your love with others.
verses.discussion_question: If you were picking a team for something important, what would you look for?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think Jesus chose twelve? How does it feel to know that Jesus 'chose' us too through faith?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one person you can invite to be on 'Jesus' team' by telling them about Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: Dawn breaking over a hillside as Jesus stands with arms open, twelve men gathering around Him from different directions, faces showing wonder and honour at being chosen. Warm sunrise colours.

verses.kjv: Simon, (whom he also named Peter,) and Andrew his brother, James and John, Philip and Bartholomew,
verses.little_bible: Here are the first six: Simon — whom Jesus also called Peter — and his brother Andrew, then James and John, and Philip and Bartholomew.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The first six were Simon Peter, Andrew, James, John, Philip, and Bartholomew.
verses.meaning: Each name is a real person with a real story. Jesus knew them all by name. He still knows every one of His followers by name today.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knows you by name.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You know my name. Thank You for calling me to follow You.
verses.discussion_question: Do you know what your name means? Why do you think names are important to God?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus named Simon 'Peter' (meaning Rock). Has God ever changed how you see yourself through His Word?
verses.do_it_today: Look up the meaning of your name and ask God to show you who He is making you to be.
verses.illustration_prompt: Six men standing together in the morning light on a hillside — fishermen and ordinary people with different faces and expressions — looking at Jesus with reverence and joy. Warm sunrise tones.

verses.kjv: Matthew and Thomas, James the son of Alphaeus, and Simon called Zelotes,
verses.little_bible: The next four were Matthew, Thomas, James the son of Alphaeus, and Simon who was called the Zealot.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Then came Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Simon the Zealot.
verses.meaning: This list includes a tax collector (Matthew), a doubter (Thomas), and a political radical (the Zealot). Jesus chose people of every personality and background.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus chooses all kinds of people.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that You can use me exactly as I am.
verses.discussion_question: If someone very different from you was on your team, how would you treat them?
verses.family_discussion: Matthew and the Zealot would have been enemies in normal life. What does it mean that Jesus put them both on His team?
verses.do_it_today: Make friends with someone today who is different from you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Four very different men — different builds, expressions, backgrounds — standing together with the others, a mix of personalities united by one calling. Warm hillside morning light.

verses.kjv: And Judas the brother of James, and Judas Iscariot, which also was the traitor.
verses.little_bible: Last came Judas the brother of James, and Judas Iscariot — the one who would later betray Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The last two were Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot who later betrayed Jesus.
verses.meaning: Even knowing that Judas would betray Him, Jesus chose him. Jesus opens His arms wide — even to those who will walk away. The door is always open.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus gives everyone a chance.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You never gave up on me. Help me never give up on others.
verses.discussion_question: Has a friend ever let you down? How did it feel? How did you handle it?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knew Judas would betray Him and still chose him. What does this teach us about how Jesus loves people?
verses.do_it_today: Forgive someone today who has let you down — even if just in your heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: The last two men stepping into the group on the hillside — one with an open, humble face, the other with a shadowed, uncertain expression. A bittersweet morning scene with the larger group in the background.

verses.kjv: And he came down with them, and stood in the plain, and the company of his disciples, and a great multitude of people out of all Judaea and Jerusalem, and from the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, which came to hear him, and to be healed of their diseases;
verses.little_bible: Jesus came down the mountain with His twelve and stood on a flat place. Huge crowds had gathered — people from all over Judaea, from Jerusalem, and even from faraway Tyre and Sidon — all coming to hear Him and be healed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus came down the mountain. Huge crowds came from far away to hear Him and be healed.
verses.meaning: News about Jesus crossed every border. People travelled long distances just to be near Him. His reputation for healing and teaching drew people like a flame draws moths.
verses.memory_phrase: People come from far away to find Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, let the news of who You are spread everywhere — starting with me telling others.
verses.discussion_question: If you heard that someone amazing was nearby, how far would you travel to meet them?
verses.family_discussion: People came from Tyre and Sidon — non-Jewish regions. What does that tell us about who Jesus came to reach?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today something amazing that Jesus has done.
verses.illustration_prompt: A vast crowd on a green plain at the foot of a mountain — people from different regions in varied clothing — all looking up at Jesus who stands calmly with His twelve nearby. Rolling hills in the distance. Epic storybook scene.

verses.kjv: And they that were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were healed.
verses.little_bible: People troubled by evil spirits also came — and they were healed too.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People with evil spirits came to Jesus. He healed them all.
verses.meaning: Jesus has authority over evil spirits just as He has authority over sickness. Nothing is too powerful for Him to overcome.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus has power over all evil.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are more powerful than anything evil. Thank You for protecting me.
verses.discussion_question: Are you ever afraid of bad or scary things? What do you do?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean that Jesus has authority over unclean spirits? How does that truth help us when we feel afraid?
verses.do_it_today: When you feel scared today, say out loud: 'Jesus, You are stronger than anything that frightens me.'
verses.illustration_prompt: People who look troubled or burdened approaching Jesus, and one by one their faces becoming peaceful and relieved. Soft light, calm watercolour tones.

verses.kjv: And the whole multitude sought to touch him: for there went virtue out of him, and healed them all.
verses.little_bible: Everyone in the crowd was trying to touch Jesus — because power was going out from Him, and He healed them all!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Everyone wanted to touch Jesus. Power came from Him and healed them all!
verses.meaning: Healing power flowed from Jesus constantly. He didn't run out. He didn't get tired of people's needs. He healed them all — every single one.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus never runs out of power.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You have endless power and love. Touch my life with Your goodness today.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen someone help and help and help without stopping? How did that make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus healed 'all' — not just some. What does that tell us about how Jesus sees people who come to Him in need?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one person who needs healing today. Ask Jesus to touch them with His power.
verses.illustration_prompt: A sea of hands reaching out toward Jesus on the plain, golden light radiating from Him, people being healed and straightening up with joy across the crowd. Breathtaking storybook panorama.

verses.kjv: And he lifted up his eyes on his disciples, and said, Blessed are ye poor: for yours is the kingdom of God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus looked right at His disciples and said: 'Blessed are you who are poor — the kingdom of God belongs to you!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Happy are those who are poor — God's kingdom is yours!'
verses.meaning: This is a radical, upside-down blessing. The kingdom is not for the powerful and wealthy — it belongs to those who know they have nothing without God.
verses.memory_phrase: God's kingdom belongs to the poor.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me know how much I need You, no matter what I own.
verses.discussion_question: Do you think having lots of things makes someone happy? Why or why not?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus said 'poor' not 'poor in spirit' like Matthew's version. Who did He mean? What does it mean that the kingdom is theirs?
verses.do_it_today: Give something you own to someone who has less today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus on the plain, eyes lifted and glowing with warmth, looking directly at a group of humble, plainly dressed disciples and poor people around Him, the light of blessing falling on their upturned faces. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Blessed are ye that hunger now: for ye shall be filled. Blessed are ye that weep now: for ye shall laugh.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Blessed are you who are hungry now — you will be filled! Blessed are you who are crying now — you will laugh!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Those who are hungry will be full. Those who cry will one day laugh!'
verses.meaning: These blessings are not about pretending pain doesn't exist — they are promises that Jesus will reverse every sorrow. Tears now; laughter is coming.
verses.memory_phrase: Tears now, laughter is coming.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I am sad and hungry for something better, remind me that You will fill me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever cried about something and then later felt better? What helped?
verses.family_discussion: How is it comforting to know that Jesus specifically blesses people who are hurting right now?
verses.do_it_today: If someone is crying or sad today, sit with them. Don't just fix it — be there.
verses.illustration_prompt: Split scene: on the left, a sad child with tears; on the right, the same child laughing joyfully in sunshine. Jesus blessing both scenes with His presence. Gentle, hopeful watercolour.

verses.kjv: Blessed are ye, when men shall hate you, and when they shall separate you from their company, and shall reproach you, and cast out your name as evil, for the Son of man's sake.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Blessed are you when people hate you, leave you out, speak against you, and call you bad names — all because you belong to the Son of Man!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You are blessed when people are mean to you because you follow Me.'
verses.meaning: Following Jesus may cost friendships and reputation. But Jesus calls that very suffering a blessing — because it aligns you with the prophets and with Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Standing with Jesus is always worth it.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when people are unkind because I follow You, help me stand firm and trust You.
verses.discussion_question: Has anyone ever made fun of you for believing in Jesus? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus says being excluded for His sake is a blessing. How can your family support each other when this happens?
verses.do_it_today: Stand up for what is true and right today, even if it is not popular.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child standing alone while a group walks away from them — but overhead a gentle beam of golden light rests on the child who stands firm. Contrast of loneliness and divine favour. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Rejoice ye in that day, and leap for joy: for, behold, your reward is great in heaven: for in the like manner did their fathers unto the prophets.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'When that happens, jump for joy! Your reward in heaven is enormous! That is how people treated the prophets who came before you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Jump for joy! Your reward in heaven is huge!'
verses.meaning: Suffering for Jesus is not the end of the story — it is the beginning of a great reward. Persecution puts you in good company with the prophets.
verses.memory_phrase: Heaven holds your reward.
verses.prayer: Lord, when things are hard, help me remember the great reward waiting for me in heaven.
verses.discussion_question: What is the biggest prize you have ever won or earned? What was it worth to you?
verses.family_discussion: How does thinking about heaven's reward change the way we handle hard times on earth?
verses.do_it_today: Write down something difficult you are going through and add: 'My reward is great in heaven.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child leaping joyfully on a hillside with arms wide, golden light raining down, a faint outline of a vast heavenly city shimmering in the clouds above. Joyful, bright watercolour.

verses.kjv: But woe unto you that are rich! for ye have received your consolation.
verses.little_bible: But then Jesus said: 'Woe to you who are rich! You have already had your comfort.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus warned: 'Woe to those who are rich — they already have all they'll get.'
verses.meaning: This is a sober warning. Those who find all their comfort in wealth have made riches their god — and riches are a poor substitute for the eternal kingdom.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't trust in riches more than God.
verses.prayer: God, help me never love money more than I love You.
verses.discussion_question: Can things and money make someone truly happy? What can't they give us?
verses.family_discussion: How do we enjoy the good things God gives without trusting in them more than in God?
verses.do_it_today: Look at the things you own and say to God: 'You are more important to me than all of this.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A wealthy person surrounded by gold and fine robes looking satisfied but hollow — while in the background simple people gather around Jesus with genuine joy. Contrasting scenes. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Woe unto you that are full! for ye shall hunger. Woe unto you that laugh now! for ye shall mourn and weep.
verses.little_bible: Jesus warned: 'Woe to those who are full — you will go hungry! Woe to those who laugh without care right now — you will mourn and cry!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Those who are full and laugh without caring for others will one day be empty and sad.'
verses.meaning: Earthly fullness without God is temporary. The laughter of those who ignore God's ways will eventually give way to grief. This is not a cruel prediction — it is a call to turn around now.
verses.memory_phrase: Only God fills us forever.
verses.prayer: Lord, fill me with what truly lasts. Keep me from living only for now.
verses.discussion_question: What is something that felt great in the moment but didn't make you happy for long?
verses.family_discussion: How do we make sure that the joy in our family is rooted in God and not just in good circumstances?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God today for something that has lasted — His love, His Word, His promises.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two images side by side: a table full of people feasting and laughing carelessly, then the same table empty and the people looking lost. A gentle, sobering contrast. Storybook illustration.

verses.kjv: Woe unto you, when all men shall speak well of you! for so did their fathers to the false prophets.
verses.little_bible: Jesus also said: 'Woe to you when everyone praises you and says only nice things about you — that is how people treated the false prophets!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Woe to you when everyone always says you're great — that was true of the fake prophets.'
verses.meaning: Being universally popular is not the goal of following Jesus. The true prophets were often rejected; the false ones were celebrated. Popularity with everyone can be a warning sign.
verses.memory_phrase: Truth matters more than popularity.
verses.prayer: God, help me care more about Your approval than what everyone else thinks of me.
verses.discussion_question: Is it possible to always try to make everyone happy? What happens when you try?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family encourage each other to do what is right even when it is not popular?
verses.do_it_today: Do something good today that no one else will see or praise you for.
verses.illustration_prompt: A crowd clapping and cheering for a smiling figure who speaks only what people want to hear, while in the corner stands a quiet prophet speaking truth that no one wants to listen to. Thoughtful scene.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you,
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said something that shocked everyone: 'I tell you — love your enemies. Do good to the people who hate you!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Love your enemies. Do good to people who hate you!'
verses.meaning: This is the most radical, counter-cultural command Jesus ever gave. Every human instinct says to love friends and fight enemies. Jesus says: love both. This changes everything.
verses.memory_phrase: Love your enemies.
verses.prayer: Jesus, this is hard. Help me love even the people who are unkind to me.
verses.discussion_question: Who is the hardest person for you to be kind to? How could you show them love?
verses.family_discussion: Why is loving enemies so hard? How does knowing Jesus loves us — even when we were His enemies — make it possible?
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind thing today for someone who has not been kind to you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus on the plain, speaking with fire in His eyes and warmth in His hands, the crowd leaning in shocked but captivated. In one corner, two people who look like they don't get along are starting to look at each other differently. Vivid storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Bless them that curse you, and pray for them which despitefully use you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'Speak well of people who say bad things about you. Pray for the people who treat you cruelly.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Say kind things about people who are mean. Pray for those who hurt you.'
verses.meaning: Blessing those who curse us breaks the cycle of anger and retaliation. Prayer for those who hurt us changes both them and us.
verses.memory_phrase: Pray for those who hurt you.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me pray for the people who hurt me. That is really hard — but I trust You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever prayed for someone who was unkind to you? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: What does praying for someone who hurt you do for your own heart, not just for theirs?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one person today who has hurt or frustrated you — and mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child kneeling to pray with eyes closed, and in the warm glow above them, a silhouette of the person who hurt them is softened with light. Gentle, healing watercolour.

verses.kjv: And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek offer also the other; and him that taketh away thy cloke forbid not to take thy coat also.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'If someone hits you on one cheek, turn and offer the other one. If someone takes your coat, let them have your shirt too.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Don't fight back. Be even more generous than what is taken from you.'
verses.meaning: Jesus is not teaching weakness — He is teaching a power that breaks cycles of retaliation. Unexpected generosity in the face of aggression is disarming and transforming.
verses.memory_phrase: Respond to harm with unexpected kindness.
verses.prayer: God, give me the strength to respond to unkindness with unexpected generosity.
verses.discussion_question: When someone is mean to you, what is your first instinct? What does Jesus say to do instead?
verses.family_discussion: This does not mean letting people keep hurting us — so what does Jesus mean? How do we understand this wisely?
verses.do_it_today: If someone is rude to you today, respond with an unexpected act of kindness.
verses.illustration_prompt: A person offering both hands open in a gesture of peace to someone who looks surprised and ashamed at their own aggression. Disarming, gentle scene. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: Give to every man that asketh of thee; and of him that taketh away thy goods ask them not again.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Give to everyone who asks you for something. If someone takes something of yours, don't demand it back.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Give to everyone who asks. If something is taken, let it go.'
verses.meaning: Jesus is calling us to open-handed generosity — holding our possessions loosely and caring more about people than things.
verses.memory_phrase: Hold what you own loosely.
verses.prayer: God, help me be generous. Things are just things — people matter more.
verses.discussion_question: Is it hard to share things you love? Why?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is not saying be irresponsible — He is saying be generous. How can your family grow in open-handed giving?
verses.do_it_today: Give something away today to someone who needs it more than you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Open hands giving something away freely — bread, a coat, coins — while the face of the giver is peaceful and smiling. Simple, generous scene. Warm tones.

verses.kjv: And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise.
verses.little_bible: Jesus gave the golden rule: 'Treat other people exactly the same way you want them to treat you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Treat others the way you want to be treated.'
verses.meaning: The Golden Rule is the simplest summary of how to live with others. If every person asked 'how would I want to be treated right now?' before acting, the world would be transformed.
verses.memory_phrase: Treat others how you want to be treated.
verses.prayer: Jesus, before I act or speak today, help me ask: would I want someone to do this to me?
verses.discussion_question: How do you want people to treat you? Are you treating others that way?
verses.family_discussion: How would our home be different if everyone followed the Golden Rule all day today?
verses.do_it_today: Before you do or say anything to someone today, ask: 'Is this how I want to be treated?' Then act.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two children facing each other with warm smiles, one offering a hand to help, a golden glow between them representing the Golden Rule. Simple, bright, cheerful storybook scene with sun streaming in.

verses.kjv: For if ye love them which love you, what thank have ye? for sinners also love those that love them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'If you only love people who love you back, why should you get any credit for that? Even people who don't follow God love their friends!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Anyone can love friends. That's not special.'
verses.meaning: Loving only those who love us back is natural — even animals do it. Jesus is calling us to a supernatural love that goes far beyond the ordinary.
verses.memory_phrase: Love more than just your friends.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me love beyond the easy people — to love even those who don't love me back.
verses.discussion_question: Is it easy to be kind to your best friends? What about people you don't know?
verses.family_discussion: What is the difference between natural human love and the love Jesus is calling us to?
verses.do_it_today: Be extra kind today to someone who has never done anything for you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two groups: one showing friends laughing together easily; another showing a child reaching out to someone who looks away or ignores them. Gentle, honest scene.

verses.kjv: And if ye do good to them which do good to you, what thank have ye? for sinners also do even the same.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'And if you do good only to people who do good to you, what credit does that get you? Anyone does that!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Doing good only to those who do good to you is nothing special.'
verses.meaning: Jesus keeps raising the bar. The world's version of goodness — scratching someone's back because they scratch yours — is not Kingdom goodness.
verses.memory_phrase: Go beyond the expected.
verses.prayer: God, help me do good without expecting anything in return.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever done something nice because you thought someone would do something nice back? Was that real kindness?
verses.family_discussion: What is the difference between doing good to get something and doing good because it is good?
verses.do_it_today: Do one good deed today with no expectation of being thanked or repaid.
verses.illustration_prompt: Someone helping another person and waving away a 'thank you' — pure generosity with no strings. Clean, warm, storybook style.

verses.kjv: And if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive, what thank have ye? for sinners also lend to sinners, to receive as much again.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'And if you lend money only to people who will pay you back, what credit is that? Everyone does that!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Lending only when you'll get it back — anyone does that.'
verses.meaning: Generosity that expects returns is not generosity — it is investment. Jesus calls us to give freely, the way God gives to us.
verses.memory_phrase: Give freely, like God gives to you.
verses.prayer: God, help me give and not always keep track of what I get back.
verses.discussion_question: Do you ever lend things only to people you know will give them back? How could you be more generous?
verses.family_discussion: What does giving without expecting anything back look like in your family's daily life?
verses.do_it_today: Lend or give something to someone today without keeping score.
verses.illustration_prompt: One person handing something to another with an open, trusting face — not watching to make sure they get it back. Peaceful, free scene. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: But love ye your enemies, and do good, and lend, hoping for nothing again: and your reward shall be great, and ye shall be the children of the Highest: for he is kind unto the unthankful and to the evil.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Love your enemies. Do good and lend without hoping for anything back. Your reward will be huge — and you will be children of the Most High God! Because God Himself is kind even to ungrateful and evil people.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Love enemies. Give with no strings. Your reward will be great. You'll be God's children!'
verses.meaning: This is the summit of Jesus' teaching on enemy love. The reason to love enemies is that God loves ungrateful and evil people — and we become like our Father when we do the same.
verses.memory_phrase: Be like your Father — kind to everyone.
verses.prayer: Father, I want to be like You. Help me love people the way You love me — even when I didn't deserve it.
verses.discussion_question: God is kind even to people who ignore Him. How does that make you feel about God?
verses.family_discussion: Verse 35 says loving enemies makes us children of the Most High. How does being God's child change how we treat people?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone who has not treated you well. Do something kind for them today as an act of worship.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with arms wide open against a sunrise sky, light streaming down from above, with the words 'child of the Most High' captured in the golden rays. Warm, triumphant watercolour.

verses.kjv: Be ye therefore merciful, as your Father also is merciful.
verses.little_bible: Jesus summed it up: 'Be merciful to others — just as your heavenly Father is merciful to you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Be as kind and forgiving as your Father in heaven is.'
verses.meaning: Mercy is the heart of God's character. To be merciful like the Father is the goal of the whole Christian life — full of compassion that doesn't give people what they deserve but gives them grace instead.
verses.memory_phrase: Be merciful as your Father is merciful.
verses.prayer: Heavenly Father, You are so merciful to me. Help me show that same mercy to everyone I meet.
verses.discussion_question: What is mercy? Can you think of a time when someone showed you mercy?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing God's mercy to us change how we treat others who fail or hurt us?
verses.do_it_today: When someone makes a mistake today, respond with mercy instead of anger.
verses.illustration_prompt: A parent with arms wrapped around a child in a warm, forgiving embrace — sunlight falling softly over them both. A picture of mercy. Tender, golden storybook scene.

verses.kjv: Judge not, and ye shall not be judged: condemn not, and ye shall not be condemned: forgive, and ye shall be forgiven:
verses.little_bible: Jesus taught: 'Don't judge other people, and you won't be judged. Don't condemn others, and you won't be condemned. Forgive others, and you will be forgiven.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Don't judge. Don't condemn. Forgive — and you will be forgiven.'
verses.meaning: Jesus gives a simple principle: the way we treat others comes back to us. Forgiveness is not optional for the person who has received God's forgiveness.
verses.memory_phrase: Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me not to be quick to judge. Help me to forgive as freely as You have forgiven me.
verses.discussion_question: Is there someone you have been judging or holding a grudge against? What is stopping you from forgiving them?
verses.family_discussion: What is the difference between judging and discerning? How do we make wise decisions without judging people harshly?
verses.do_it_today: Let go of one judgment you have been holding about someone. Choose to forgive.
verses.illustration_prompt: Scales of justice — but instead of weighing crimes, one side holds a heart being released in forgiveness, and light floods the scene. Symbolic and bright. Storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: Give, and it shall be given unto you; good measure, pressed down, and shaken together, and running over, shall men give into your bosom. For with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Give — and it will come back to you! Not just a little — pressed down, shaken together, and running over into your arms. Because how you measure out for others is how God will measure back to you!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Give generously, and God will give back even more — overflowing!'
verses.meaning: Generosity is not a loss — it opens a door for God's abundant blessing. The image is of grain heaped into a basket until it spills over. God gives back more than we give out.
verses.memory_phrase: Give generously — God gives back overflowing.
verses.prayer: God, help me give generously and trust You to fill me back up — pressed down and running over!
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever given something away and then received something even better? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: How does this verse change the way we think about giving — to church, to others, to the poor?
verses.do_it_today: Give something generously today — time, money, or kindness — and trust God with the rest.
verses.illustration_prompt: A basket being filled with golden grain, pressed down and shaken, then overflowing onto the ground — warm, abundant, joyful. A person's arms open wide to receive it. Rich storybook watercolour colours.

verses.kjv: And he spake a parable unto them, Can the blind lead the blind? shall they not both fall into the ditch?
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them a parable: 'Can a blind person lead another blind person? Of course not — they will both fall into a ditch!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'Can a blind person lead a blind person? No — they'd both fall in a hole!'
verses.meaning: Before you lead others, you must be able to see clearly yourself. We cannot take people where we haven't gone. Spiritual sight comes from following Jesus closely.
verses.memory_phrase: Follow someone who can truly see.
verses.prayer: Jesus, open my eyes so I can clearly follow You and help others follow too.
verses.discussion_question: What makes a good leader? Who are the best leaders in your life?
verses.family_discussion: This is a warning about following teachers who are spiritually blind. How do we discern who to learn from?
verses.do_it_today: Follow someone wise today — ask a parent or mentor for guidance on something you're uncertain about.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two people with eyes closed walking together toward a ditch, while in the background a person with open eyes leads others safely along a lit path. Clear contrast. Storybook illustration.

verses.kjv: The disciple is not above his master: but every one that is perfect shall be as his master.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'A student is not greater than his teacher — but when fully trained, the student will be just like his teacher.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Students become like their teachers. Follow the right teacher!'
verses.meaning: We become what we learn. If we follow Jesus closely and learn from Him, we become more like Him — which is the whole point of being a disciple.
verses.memory_phrase: Disciples become like their master.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to become more and more like You every day. Teach me.
verses.discussion_question: Who do you learn from the most? How are you becoming like them?
verses.family_discussion: If following Jesus means becoming like Him, what areas of your life are still needing to look more like Him?
verses.do_it_today: Study one thing Jesus did today and try to do it yourself.
verses.illustration_prompt: A young student watching carefully as a skilled teacher works, then the student doing the same thing confidently. A picture of learning and becoming. Warm and encouraging storybook scene.

verses.kjv: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother's eye, but perceivest not the beam that is in thine own eye?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked: 'Why do you stare at the tiny speck in your friend's eye, but you don't even notice the huge log stuck in your own eye?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'Why notice a speck in someone's eye but miss the log in your own?'
verses.meaning: It is always easier to see others' faults than our own. Jesus calls us to self-examination before criticism. The plank and the speck are a comic, unforgettable picture.
verses.memory_phrase: Check your own eye first.
verses.prayer: Lord, show me my own faults before I point out others'. Give me humble eyes.
verses.discussion_question: Is it easier to notice when you do something wrong, or when someone else does? Why?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean in practical terms to 'take the plank out of your own eye' before helping someone else?
verses.do_it_today: Before you correct someone today, ask yourself: 'Do I have the same problem?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A comic and gentle watercolour of someone squinting at a tiny speck in another's eye while an enormous wooden plank sticks out of their own eye. Playful but instructive. Bright colours.

verses.kjv: Either how canst thou say to thy brother, Brother, let me pull out the mote that is in thine eye, when thou thyself beholdest not the beam that is in thine own eye? Thou hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine own eye, and then shalt thou see clearly to pull out the mote that is in thy brother's eye.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'How can you say, "Let me help you with that speck" when you have a whole log in your own eye? Hypocrite! First take the log out of your own eye. Then you will see clearly enough to help your friend.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Fix yourself first. Then you can help others clearly.'
verses.meaning: Jesus is not saying never help others — He is saying the order matters. Personal honesty and growth come first. Then, with clear vision, we can genuinely help.
verses.memory_phrase: Fix yourself first, then help others.
verses.prayer: Lord, make me honest about my own faults. Then help me to gently help others.
verses.discussion_question: What is a hypocrite? Have you ever met one? Have you ever been one?
verses.family_discussion: How can we gently help each other in our family while also staying humble about our own faults?
verses.do_it_today: Confess one fault to God today honestly before you think about correcting anyone else.
verses.illustration_prompt: Someone carefully removing a giant wooden plank from their own eye first — expression of humility and relief — then turning to gently help someone else with a tiny speck. Sequence of images. Warm tones.

verses.kjv: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit; neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'A healthy tree doesn't produce bad fruit. And a sick tree can't produce good fruit.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Good trees make good fruit. Bad trees make bad fruit.'
verses.meaning: What we produce in life comes from what we are on the inside. Behaviour flows from character. The answer to better fruit is not trying harder — it is becoming a healthier tree.
verses.memory_phrase: Good character grows good fruit.
verses.prayer: Lord, make me a healthy tree. Grow good things in me from the inside out.
verses.discussion_question: Can you make an apple tree grow grapes? What does that tell us about how character works?
verses.family_discussion: What kind of 'fruit' is your family producing? What kind of 'tree' do you want to be?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to work on the inside of you — your heart — not just your behaviour.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two trees side by side: a lush, healthy tree with bright fruit hanging heavy on its branches, and a gnarled, dry tree with shrivelled or no fruit. Simple, vivid storybook illustration.

verses.kjv: For every tree is known by his own fruit. For of thorns men do not gather figs, nor of a bramble bush gather they grapes.
verses.little_bible: Jesus explained: 'You always know a tree by the fruit it grows. You can't pick figs from a thornbush. You don't harvest grapes from a briar patch.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You know a tree by its fruit. Thorns don't make figs!'
verses.meaning: People are known by what they produce in life. Actions reveal the heart. There is no disguising a bad tree if you look at its fruit long enough.
verses.memory_phrase: You are known by what you produce.
verses.prayer: God, let the fruit of my life show clearly that I belong to You.
verses.discussion_question: How do you know if someone is truly kind? What do you look at?
verses.family_discussion: What does the fruit of your family's life say about who you are? What would you like it to say?
verses.do_it_today: Do something today that is 'good fruit' — something that shows who you really are in Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child reaching into a thorn bush and looking puzzled — then looking at a fig tree heavy with ripe fruit in contrast. Simple, cheerful watercolour teaching illustration.

verses.kjv: A good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is good; and an evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is evil: for of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaketh.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'A good person brings good things out of the good stored in their heart. An evil person brings evil things out of the evil stored in theirs. Because what your mouth says comes from what is filling your heart.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'What is in your heart comes out of your mouth. Fill it with good things!'
verses.meaning: Our words are a window into our hearts. If we want to speak better, we need hearts that are filled with better things — God's Word, His love, His truth.
verses.memory_phrase: Your words show what fills your heart.
verses.prayer: Lord, fill my heart so full of good things that only good things come out of my mouth.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever said something you wish you hadn't? Where did those words come from?
verses.family_discussion: What are we filling our hearts with — the things we watch, listen to, talk about? How does that change what comes out?
verses.do_it_today: Fill your heart with something good today — a worship song, a Bible verse, a prayer — and notice what comes out of your mouth.
verses.illustration_prompt: A transparent heart with golden treasure glowing inside, and kind, bright words floating up from it. Beside it, a heart filled with dark clutter with harsh words coming out. Vivid visual contrast. Storybook.

verses.kjv: And why call ye me, Lord, Lord, and do not the things which I say?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked: 'Why do you call Me "Lord, Lord" — but then not actually do what I tell you?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'Why do you call Me Lord but don't do what I say?'
verses.meaning: This is a piercing question. Many people call Jesus Lord with their lips but not with their lives. Real discipleship is not words — it is obedience.
verses.memory_phrase: Call Jesus Lord with your life, not just your lips.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I don't just want to say You are Lord — I want to live like You are Lord. Help me obey.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to really mean what you say?
verses.family_discussion: Is there a gap in your family between what you say you believe and how you actually live? What is one step to close that gap?
verses.do_it_today: Do one specific thing Jesus has told you to do that you have been putting off.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child saying 'Yes, Lord!' with their mouth while their feet point a different direction — then a second image of the same child walking toward Jesus with purposeful, obedient steps. Honest and hopeful.

verses.kjv: Whosoever cometh to me, and heareth my sayings, and doeth them, I will shew you to whom he is like:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I want to show you what someone is like who comes to Me, listens to My words, and actually does them.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Let Me show you what someone who hears Me and obeys is like.'
verses.meaning: Jesus is about to tell one of the most famous parables in the Gospels. The key is the combination: coming to Jesus, hearing His words, AND doing them. All three together.
verses.memory_phrase: Come, hear, and do — all three.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me come to You, hear You, and actually do what You say — every day.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between hearing something and actually doing it?
verses.family_discussion: How does your family make sure you don't just hear God's Word but actually put it into practice?
verses.do_it_today: Pick one thing Jesus has taught in Luke 6 today and put it into action before the day ends.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus leaning forward with a storyteller's excitement in His eyes, about to share a vivid picture, the crowd leaning in to listen. Anticipation in the air. Warm, inviting storybook scene.

verses.kjv: He is like a man which built an house, and digged deep, and laid the foundation on a rock: and when the flood arose, the stream beat vehemently upon that house, and could not shake it: for it was founded upon a rock.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'That person is like a man who built a house — but first he dug down deep, all the way to solid rock, and laid his foundation there. When the floods came and the river pounded against that house, it did not shake even a little — because it was built on rock!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The wise man dug deep and built on rock. When floods came, the house stood firm!
verses.meaning: Building on Jesus' words takes effort — digging deep, not taking shortcuts. But when the storms of life come (and they will come), that foundation holds firm. This is the promise.
verses.memory_phrase: Build your life on Jesus — and it will stand.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me dig deep and build my life on Your words. I want to stand firm in every storm.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen a building with a really deep foundation? Why does digging deep matter?
verses.family_discussion: What storms have you been through as a family? What helped your house stand? How can you build a deeper foundation in Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that is 'digging deep' in your faith — pray longer, read more, obey something hard.
verses.illustration_prompt: A builder digging deep into solid rock with a shovel, sweat on his brow, determined — while the foundation stones are being laid strong and true. In the background, a storm is gathering on the horizon. Dramatic, vivid storybook watercolour. Rich colours.

verses.kjv: But he that heareth, and doeth not, is like a man that without a foundation built an house upon the earth; against which the stream did beat vehemently, and immediately it fell; and the ruin of that house was great.
verses.little_bible: Jesus warned: 'But the person who hears My words and does nothing about them is like a man who built a house on bare dirt with no foundation at all. When the river came and beat against it — immediately, it crashed to the ground! And what a terrible ruin it was!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The foolish man built on dirt. When the storm came, the house crashed down immediately!
verses.meaning: Hearing without obeying is dangerous. The foolish builder did not lay no foundation by accident — he simply did not bother to dig. Laziness in building spiritual foundations has catastrophic results.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't just hear — obey!
verses.prayer: Lord, I don't want to just hear Your words. I want to build my life on them. Help me obey.
verses.discussion_question: What would happen if you built a sandcastle and a wave came? How is that like a life built without Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus ends His sermon with this warning — why? What is the most important takeaway from Luke 6 for your family?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God: 'Is there any part of my life that is not built on Your words?' Then listen — and act.
verses.illustration_prompt: A poorly built house on bare muddy earth being hit by a raging flood, walls crumbling, roof collapsing, water rushing in from all sides — dramatic and vivid. The contrast to verse 48's solid house is stark. Dramatic storybook watercolour with dark storm skies and crashing water.
